response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web-focused engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ usage is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years required. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no exposure to fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets systems. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and distributed systems, which does not transfer to the mandatory requirements here.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) relevant to high-quality delivery in critical systems
Broad experience across low-latency and high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of requests/sec)
Active communicator and community contributor — workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects indicate strong verbal and written communication
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project) versus the 5–10 years mandated
No capital markets, investment banking, fixed income, or financial-products domain knowledge whatsoever
No evidence of Windows-platform development or SQL Server/Oracle usage — both are hard requirements
